audience: students, privacy advocates, security engineers, developers
title: AegisVault (StealthNotes)
summary: A stealth, offline-first notes application disguised as a normal productivity tool with an encrypted SQLCipher vault and gesture-based decoy pattern unlock.
overview: AegisVault is an advanced Android security project written in Kotlin and Jetpack Compose. On the surface, it functions as a lightweight notes and document organizer. Beneath the unencrypted public layer lies a highly secure database (SQLCipher) and file vault encrypted with AES-256-CBC. Access is triggered completely invisibly through custom swipe gestures on a gateway note, offering robust plausible deniability.
longDescription: Developed to address the growing demand for deep mobile privacy, AegisVault is a stealth-oriented notes and document security repository for Android. It operates as a dual-layer architecture: a fully functional, public note-taking layout (unencrypted SQLite via Room) that anyone can browse, and a hidden secure vault (SQLCipher database) accessible only through an invisible gesture pattern. The encryption keys are derived dynamically from the user's custom swipe pattern using PBKDF2 with 10,000 iterations, ensuring that no raw passphrases or keys are hardcoded in memory or storage. Additionally, it implements failed unlock attempt tracking, automatic lock on backgrounding, and an optional emergency panic wipe trigger. This is an exceptional reference project for students studying mobile cryptography, secure database access, and advanced custom canvas gestures in Jetpack Compose.
features: Stealth Security Veil: Boring notes wrapper design carrying zero security branding to avoid user suspicion
Dual-Layer Database: Independent Room database for public notes alongside an AES-256 SQLCipher vault database
Invisible Gateway Note: Custom canvas monitoring swipe sequences invisibly over what looks like an empty notes pad
Dynamic PBKDF2 Passphrase: Advanced cryptographic key derivation utilizing 10,000 iterations from gesture hashes
Gesture Pattern Encoder: Robust gesture manager checking swipe directions, length thresholds, and consecutive boundaries
Failed Attempt Tracking: Dynamic system logging authentication attempts, triggering decoy messages or locker counts
Emergency Auto-Wipe: Smart security option allowing absolute deletion of the secure vault upon exceeding failed inputs
Local-First Architecture: Complete local storage operation requiring zero network permissions, preventing any packet leaks
Secure Memory Clearing: Cryptographic wrapper wiping active byte arrays and credentials instantly from system RAM after use
Material Design 3 Theme: Clean, modern Jetpack Compose theme featuring responsive typography, micro-animations, and layouts
Secure DataStore Preferences: Modern encrypted storage keeping track of gateway setups and failed attempt configurations
deliverables: Kotlin Android source code (Jetpack Compose, MVVM)
Complete compilation guide and presentation briefs
